description: Returns the list of supported GDAL raster drivers and file formats.
Opens a raster file (or a mosaic of raster files) and returns a table with the raster data. Supports filter pushdown on all non-BLOB columns.
Exports a data table to a new raster file.
example: COPY ( SELECT geometry, databand_1 FROM RT_Read('path/to/raster/file.tif') ) TO 'path/to/raster/newfile.tif' WITH (FORMAT RASTER, DRIVER 'COG'...);

description: Packages a plain SQL array of numeric values back into a datacube BLOB, the inverse of RT_Cube2Array.
example: SELECT RT_Array2Cube(r.values, 'RAW', r.bands, r.cols, r.rows, r.no_data) FROM ...;

description: Extracts the pixel values of a datacube column into a plain SQL array of a chosen numeric type. Optionally filters out nodata values. A different function variant is provided for each numeric data type (e.g. RT_Cube2ArrayInt32, RT_Cube2ArrayFloat, etc.).
example: SELECT RT_Cube2ArrayInt32(databand_1, true) AS r FROM RT_Read('path/to/raster/file.tif');

description: Changes the pixel data type of a datacube, returning a new datacube of the same dimensions. A different function variant is provided for each numeric data type (e.g. RT_Cube2TypeUInt8, RT_Cube2TypeFloat, etc.).
Returns a datacube with the absolute value of each cell. Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns a datacube with each cell equal to the sum of the two inputs. Inputs can be two datacubes or a datacube and a scalar. Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns a datacube where cells inside the given geometry are replaced by the specified value. Cells outside the geometry are preserved. Nodata cells are preserved.
example: SELECT RT_CubeBurn(databand_1, tile_x, tile_y, blocksize_x, blocksize_y, geo_transform, ST_GeomFromText('POLYGON((...))'), 255.0) FROM ...;

description: Returns a datacube where cells outside the given geometry are replaced by the specified value. Cells inside the geometry are preserved. Nodata cells are preserved.
example: SELECT RT_CubeClip(databand_1, tile_x, tile_y, blocksize_x, blocksize_y, geo_transform, ST_GeomFromText('POLYGON((...))'), 0.0) FROM ...;

description: Returns a datacube with each cell equal to the left-hand cell divided by the right-hand cell. Inputs can be two datacubes or a datacube and a scalar. Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns a datacube where each cell is 1 if left == right, 0 otherwise. Inputs can be two datacubes or a datacube and a scalar. Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns a datacube with the exponential (e^x) of each cell. Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns a datacube where all cells (including nodata) are unconditionally replaced by the right-hand value.
Returns a datacube where each cell is 1 if left > right, 0 otherwise. Inputs can be two datacubes or a datacube and a scalar. Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns a datacube where each cell is 1 if left >= right, 0 otherwise. Inputs can be two datacubes or a datacube and a scalar. Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns a datacube where each cell is 1 if left < right, 0 otherwise. Inputs can be two datacubes or a datacube and a scalar. Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns a datacube where each cell is 1 if left <= right, 0 otherwise. Inputs can be two datacubes or a datacube and a scalar. Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns a datacube with the natural logarithm of each cell. Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns a datacube with each cell equal to the maximum of the two inputs. Inputs can be two datacubes or a datacube and a scalar. Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns a datacube with each cell equal to the minimum of the two inputs. Inputs can be two datacubes or a datacube and a scalar. Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns a datacube with each cell equal to the remainder of dividing the left-hand cell by the right-hand value. Inputs can be two datacubes or a datacube and a scalar. Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns a datacube with each cell equal to the product of the two inputs. Inputs can be two datacubes or a datacube and a scalar. Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns a datacube with each cell negated (multiplied by -1). Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns a datacube where each cell is 1 if left != right, 0 otherwise. Inputs can be two datacubes or a datacube and a scalar. Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns true if the datacube is NULL or contains only nodata cells, false otherwise.
Vectorizes a datacube by creating a polygon geometry for each contiguous region of non-nodata values.
example: SELECT RT_Polygon(databand_1, tile_x, tile_y, blocksize_x, blocksize_y, geo_transform) FROM ...;

description: Returns a datacube with each cell raised to the power of the right-hand value. Inputs can be two datacubes or a datacube and a scalar. Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns a datacube where valid cells are replaced by the right-hand value. Nodata cells in the source are preserved.
Returns a datacube where nodata cells are replaced by the specified value, and sets this value as the new nodata sentinel.
Returns a datacube with the square root of each cell. Nodata cells are preserved.
Calculates statistics for a specific band (0-based index) of a datacube. Returns a STRUCT with minimum, maximum, mean, stddev, valid_count and nodata_count fields.
example: SELECT RT_CubeStats(databand_1, 0) AS stats FROM RT_Read('path/to/raster/file.tif');

description: Returns a datacube with each cell equal to the left-hand cell minus the right-hand cell. Inputs can be two datacubes or a datacube and a scalar. Nodata cells are preserved.
Computes the bounding box of the valid (non-nodata) cells in the input datacube and returns it as a geometry.
example: SELECT RT_Envelope(databand_1, tile_x, tile_y, (metadata->'blocksize_x')::INTEGER, (metadata->'blocksize_y')::INTEGER, (metadata->'transform')::DOUBLE[]) FROM RT_Read('path/to/raster/file.tif');

description: Sets a GDAL configuration option (equivalent to CPLSetConfigOption). Pass NULL as the value to unset the option.
